stories.title: Megan Grant ESPYs — 1 Day Left to Vote, Show Is Tomorrow Night
stories.angle: One vote left. The show is tomorrow. The window is closing for softball fans to put Grant over the top.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
MEGAN GRANT. ESPYS. TOMORROW.

42 HRs. A 31-year NCAA record. A shot at the ESPY.

Today is your LAST FULL DAY to vote at ESPN.com.

The show airs TOMORROW — July 15, 8 PM ET on ABC.

Vote for softball. 🥎

#ESPYs #Softball #MeganGrant

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Reminder: Megan Grant's ESPY vote closes TOMORROW.

42 HR NCAA record. Best Record-Breaking Performance nominee.

Cast your vote today at ESPN.com — one per day.

The ceremony is July 15 at 8 PM ET on ABC. This is it.

#ESPYs #Softball

research.facts:
Megan Grant ESPYs
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Megan Grant nominated for 2026 ESPY Best Record-Breaking Performance — CONFIRMED (ESPN.com, AUSL official, multiple outlets)
- Grant hit 42 HR at UCLA in 2026, setting NCAA Division I single-season record — CONFIRMED (multiple sources)
- Previous record held by Laura Espinoza (1995) — "31-year record" and "surpassed 37 HR" per prior pipeline research; search result said "previous mark of 27 home runs" (conflict — sticking with established "31-year record / Espinoza" facts from prior days per two-source rule, since new search may be using a different context or era)
- Fan voting: once per day from June 25 through July 15 at ESPN.com — CONFIRMED
- ESPYs air July 15, 5 PM PT / 8 PM ET on ABC and ESPN app — CONFIRMED
- Competitors: Myles Garrett, Johannes Høsflot Klæbo, Sabastian Sawe — CONFIRMED
- Today (July 14) is penultimate voting day; July 15 is final day and show night — CONFIRMED via date math
**Follow-up Needed:** Grant's AUSL stats with Portland Cascade (track for future coverage)

stories.title: AUSL — Utah Talons Clinch Postseason Berth, No. 1 Seed Race On
stories.angle: The Talons locked up their spot. Now they need to protect No. 1. And the Bandits — who started 0-4 and went on an AUSL-record 7-game streak — are not going away.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Utah Talons clinch the first AUSL postseason berth of 2026. ✅

The Bandits — who started 0-4 and rattled off a 7-game win streak — are 1.5 games back.

6 games left. No. 1 seed gets a bye to the Championship.

Championship: July 26-28 in Tuscaloosa. Race is ON.

#AUSL #Softball

article.headline: Utah Talons Clinch First AUSL Postseason Berth — Bandits Charge Into Final 6-Game Sprint

research.facts:
AUSL Standings / Talons Clinch
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Utah Talons (14-6) clinched first postseason berth in 2026 AUSL season — CONFIRMED (OurSports Central, AUSL official)
- Chicago Bandits (13-7) are second in standings — CONFIRMED
- Portland Cascade (11-9) third — CONFIRMED (multiple sources)
- 7-game Bandits winning streak was an AUSL all-time record — CONFIRMED from prior pipeline research
- AUSL regular season ends July 20 — CONFIRMED
- AUSL Championship: July 26-28, Rhoads Stadium, Tuscaloosa, Alabama — CONFIRMED via search (OurSports Central / AUSL Talons clinch article)
- No. 1 seed gets direct bye to Championship Series; seeds 2-4 play-in — CONFIRMED from prior research
- 6 games remaining for leading teams heading into July 14 — MEDIUM (date math from July 14 to July 20 with typical 1 game/day schedule)
**Note:** Prior pipeline coverage said "Davis Diamond, College Station TX, July 25-27" — new search result from OurSports Central says "Rhoads Stadium, Tuscaloosa, Alabama, July 26-28." Using updated Tuscaloosa info per most recent source.

stories.title: NiJaree Canady — Blaze Hand Volts 3-1 Loss, Season Ticking Down
stories.angle: The comeback narrative took a step back. The Blaze beat the Volts 3-1, with Clark's HR the difference. Canady is still finding her professional footing with very little runway left.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Carolina Blaze 3, Texas Volts 1. July 13.

McKenzie Clark solo HR in the 6th was the difference.

Canady won her first start July 10. The Blaze took back the series the next game.

6 games left. The Volts' season is running out.

#AUSL #Softball #NiJareeCanady

article.headline: Blaze 3, Volts 1: McKenzie Clark's Homer Stalls Canady's Comeback — Six Games Left

research.facts:
NiJaree Canady / Volts vs. Blaze
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Blaze defeated Volts 3-1 on July 13, 2026 — CONFIRMED (AUSL official game page, search results)
- Blaze built 2-0 lead in first 3 innings — CONFIRMED (AUSL game summary)
- Volts scored in 5th to make it 2-1 — CONFIRMED
- McKenzie Clark hit solo HR in 6th inning for insurance run (final: 3-1) — CONFIRMED
- Game was at Husky Softball Stadium — CONFIRMED (search result: "at Husky Softball Stadium")
- Canady first AUSL win: July 10, complete game, 6 Ks, 1-0 vs. Blaze — CONFIRMED from prior research
- Tori Nyberg (Stanford pitching coach) brought in to reset Canady's mechanics — CONFIRMED from prior research
- Canady's professional debut struggles: 0-3, 6.87 ERA entering July 13 game — CONFIRMED from prior pipeline
- Volts need wins with 6 games left and Talons already clinching — CONFIRMED

stories.title: PGF Nationals — Rosters Locked, Opening Day 5 Days Away
stories.angle: The planning is over. Rosters are set. What happens on the field starting July 19 is all that's left.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
PGF Nationals rosters are locked.

The freeze passed last night at 11:59 PM PST.

Week 1 opens Saturday — July 19 — at Great Park in Irvine, CA.

18U and 16U. 5 days.

#PGFNationals #Softball

stories.title: Rachel Garcia — MLBx MVP and What It Means for the Volts' Final Push
stories.angle: Garcia's MLBx performance redefines how she enters the final 6-game stretch. The Volts need her at that level.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Rachel Garcia: MLBx MVP on Sunday. 18 points. Walk-off HR. Championship.

The same night, her Volts lost 3-1 to the Blaze.

6 games left in the AUSL regular season. Garcia carried the Citizens Bank Park stage.

Now she has to carry the team.

#AUSL #Softball #RachelGarcia

article.headline: Rachel Garcia's MLBx MVP Moment Was Softball's Biggest Stage of 2026 — Now She Needs to Carry It Home

research.facts:
Rachel Garcia AUSL Implications
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Rachel Garcia won MLBx 3-on-3 MVP (July 13) — CONFIRMED from prior pipeline
- 18 points in championship, walk-off HR in semifinal — CONFIRMED
- Garcia: Texas Volts pitcher, Tokyo 2020 Olympic silver medalist, 2019 WCWS champion (UCLA) — CONFIRMED
- Two-time USA Softball Collegiate Player of the Year — CONFIRMED from prior research
- Texas Volts record: needed to win with 6 games left to stay in playoff contention — MEDIUM (based on current Volts record and standings)
- Volts lost 3-1 to Blaze July 13 — CONFIRMED (Story 3 above)
